title: Satureja ascendens
taxonomy: Satureja ascendens is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. It belongs to the genus Satureja, which consists of several species of herbs commonly known as savory or summer savory. The plant is native to the Mediterranean region and is often found in dry, rocky areas. Satureja ascendens is a small shrub or herb with small, white or pinkish flowers that are highly fragrant. The plant is often used in traditional medicine and is known for its aromatic and medicinal properties.
uses: Satureja ascendens has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. It is often used to treat digestive issues, such as bloating and gas, and may also help to reduce fever. The plant has also been used to soothe skin irritations and wounds due to its antimicrobial properties. Additionally, Satureja ascendens is sometimes used to alleviate respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds. More research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential benefits of this plant.

intro: Satureja ascendens, also known as the mountain savory, is a perennial herb belonging to the Lamiaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Satureja ascendens include essential oils, flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, such as carvacrol and thymol, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In herbal medicine, Satureja ascendens is often used to make teas, infusions, and essential oils, as well as being incorporated into salves and creams for topical application.
